chief_complaint: A previously healthy male presented with acute dyspnoea, chest pain, and diaphoresis.
history: The patient had no clear history of exposure, but his symptoms suggested non-ST elevation acute coronary syndrome and pneumonia.
exam: No specific physical examination findings are provided, but the patient deteriorated, necessitating intubation.
labs: Serum acetylcholinesterase activity was markedly reduced, confirming OP poisoning as the underlying cause. ECG suggested non-ST elevation acute coronary syndrome.
